title: dimorphanthera keysseri
intro: Dimorphanthera keysseri, a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, is also known as the Keysser's bell or Keysser's bellflower. It is native to the tropical forests of the Philippines and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin infections, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Dimorphanthera keysseri is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and topical creams that can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
